violations.text: Establishment did not have a food safety manager at time of inspection. Establishment must have a food safety manager onsite at all times when time/temperature control for safety foods are being prepared. Please email inspector proof of enrollment and course completion. New Violation. Correct By: 02/04/2026

Prepackaged mini spinach strombolis were observed held at 64-65 degrees Fahrenheit on the counter. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge, mini spinach strombolis were on the counter for 2 hours. Food was relocated to the freezer to rapidly cool. Establishment must keep food under refrigeration. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/04/2026

Baked goods packaged in the food establishment are lacking label information (subingredients and quantity). Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. All food was relocated behind the counter and must not be sold as a self-service item until labels are made. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/06/2026

Bread, calzones, and mini spinach strombolis packaged in the food establishment are lacking labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. All items were relocated behind the counter and must not be sold as a self-service item until proper labels are made. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/06/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/06/2026

Calzones were observed not marked with the date of preparation in the walk-in cooler. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/06/2026

Calzones and bread packaged in the food establishment are lacking labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. Bread was relocated behind the counter during time of inspection and must not be made available for self-service until labels are made. Packaged calzones had ingredients listed on the refrigerator door. Establishment may temporarily keep calzones in the refrigerator. Labels must be placed on calzones. Please email inspector proof of correction. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/06/2026

Baked goods packaged in the food establishment are lacking label information (subingredients and quantity). Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. Owner is in the process of making new labels. Please email inspector proof of correction. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/06/2026

Prepared foods (sausage, steak, buffalo chicken, etc.) were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 01/30/2024

Baked goods packaged in the food establishment are lacking labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. New Violation. Correct By: 02/07/2024

The floor in the walk-in cooler is in poor repair. Material is warped and an accumulation of food debris observed. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair and constructed of approved material. The walls in the dough prep area are constructed of wood/plywood. This material is not durable. Materials for wall surfaces shall be smooth, durable, easily cleanable, and non-absorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware-washing areas, and toilet rooms. New Violation. Correct By: 05/31/2024

The floors and walls throughout the dough prep area have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 02/06/2024
